checkpoint: v0.107 Salto Vertical Slice Composition Plan and Asset-Dimension Contracts

composition.representativeBattleSlice: First Claim-style readable battle slice using existing map/content posture only
appearsOnScreen: ford or readable water crossing treatment
remainsPlaceholder: current procedural terrain, road, water, ruin, quarry, shrine, fog, minimap, and Lume primitives
current placeholder unit and building silhouettes
current CSS and DOM HUD, Results, and campaign frames
canEventuallyBecomeRuntimeCandidate: only after Emmanuel human style approval, source/license review, protected-IP review, and a future explicit runtime integration milestone

runtimePosture.notes: This manifest plans future art one asset at a time. It does not approve runtime loading, import paths, image generation, save changes, gameplay changes, or package metadata changes.

privateMockComposition.deferredReason: A private wireframe preview would add a new UI surface and visual QA burden. v0.107 keeps composition review in deterministic docs and packet metadata only.

qaScenarios.reviewTarget: Salto highland mood, wet roads, granite, readable lanes, ruin/quarry/shrine identity, and open UI-safe negative space.
Walkable, blocked, resource, capture, and water/ford surfaces stay readable at 1366x768 and 1920x1080.
Compact desktop RTS UI, readable resource/command/minimap hierarchy, and Results debrief framing without mobile-card drift.
One restrained teal Lume link reads as land-power, not generic magic-blue clutter.
Campaign map frame supports Salto-region launch, selected mission focus, locked-state clarity, and return-from-Results posture.
Hero, Worker, Militia, and Ranger have distinct RTS-scale silhouettes at 64x64 and 96x96 review thumbnails.
Command Hall, Barracks, mine, and shrine are distinct by footprint, roofline, material, and entry/readability cues.
Ashen contrast stays disciplined and readable without demon/orc/undead drift or overdark silhouette collapse.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 16:9 reference style frame, review candidate only
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: not applicable until future runtime integration gate
assets.cameraRule: high three-quarter RTS camera with readable lanes, buildable space, water/ford edge, quarry, shrine, and ruin silhouettes

safeCrop.rule: leave negative space for HUD and objective overlays

assets.silhouetteRule: terrain landmarks must read before atmospheric detail

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: road, ford/water, quarry, shrine, and ruin are still identifiable

assets.maximumVisualNoise: medium-low; avoid foliage speckle, fog soup, and noisy ground tiling
assets.mipDownscalePosture: future crops must be checked at 50%, 25%, and 64px landmark thumbnails

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current procedural terrain, roads, water, resource rings, and ruin blockers remain authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the frame feel like Salto wet highland terrain rather than generic fantasy grassland?
Are playable lanes and blocked landmark areas readable?
Does the image leave enough UI-safe space?
assets.notes: First reference frame; it anchors all later Salto slice art.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 16:9 battlefield review frame, no fake UI screenshot
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: not applicable until future runtime integration gate
assets.cameraRule: high three-quarter RTS battlefield camera with visible resource and objective surfaces

safeCrop.rule: do not crop capture rings, ford edges, or command-area landmarks

assets.silhouetteRule: walkable, blocked, capturable, and hostile-owned zones must separate at a glance

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: capture site, road, ford, and ruin remain legible in contact sheets

assets.maximumVisualNoise: low around units and capture sites; medium only in non-playable edge dressing
assets.animationPosture: static reference only; future runtime candidates need separate animation/VFX review
assets.mipDownscalePosture: terrain colors must retain contrast after 25% downscale

fallbackBehavior.behavior: existing procedural terrain, fog, capture rings, objective markers, and minimap markers stay in place

humanApprovalQuestions: Can a player distinguish road, ford/water, quarry, shrine, and blocked ruin immediately?
Does the battlefield remain readable with current HUD density?
Does terrain mood support Barrosan highland identity without visual clutter?
assets.notes: Gameplay map rules remain unchanged; this is only a battlefield material/composition reference.

assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future sliced UI candidates only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: flat orthographic UI board, not a fake gameplay screenshot
assets.transparencyRequirement: opaque board with clear component boundaries

safeCrop.rule: all frame examples remain fully visible without edge clipping

assets.silhouetteRule: resource, command, minimap, selected-unit, and tooltip frames must be distinguishable by shape and state

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: frame hierarchy reads without relying on small decorative detail

assets.maximumVisualNoise: low; texture supports contrast rather than competing with text
assets.animationPosture: static reference only; hover/press animation deferred
assets.mipDownscalePosture: future slices must keep 1x and 0.5x text contrast margins

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current CSS/DOM HUD and minimap frames remain authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the HUD feel Barrosan without becoming parchment-heavy or mobile-like?
Do locked, selected, available, hostile, friendly, and neutral states remain clear?
Can the frame style support Minimal public HUD density?
assets.notes: HUD frame is a reference for future UI kit work, not a CSS implementation request.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 16:9 flat debrief frame reference paired with the HUD frame
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future UI slice candidates only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: flat orthographic debrief board with no fake reward values or unapproved UI text

safeCrop.rule: primary outcome, objective summary, reward frame, and return actions remain inside safe margins

assets.silhouetteRule: victory/defeat framing, reward panel, and action area must separate without heavy ornament

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: outcome header and reward frame hierarchy read as a debrief screen

assets.maximumVisualNoise: low behind text and reward rows
assets.mipDownscalePosture: future slices must preserve button and reward-row legibility

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current CSS/DOM Results layout remains authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the debrief feel connected to the HUD without becoming a separate visual language?
Are victory, defeat, rewards, progression, and return actions easy to scan?
Is the frame quiet enough for dense results copy?
assets.notes: Documented as a HUD companion because v0.88 has one shared UI registry entry.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 16:9 or focused 4:3 Lume link reference; review candidate only
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future VFX or vector-treatment candidate only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: high three-quarter RTS camera showing one endpoint pair and link-state readability
assets.transparencyRequirement: opaque reference frame; future VFX transparency deferred

safeCrop.rule: both endpoints and the link body remain visible

assets.silhouetteRule: active, guide, contested, and severed postures must differ by more than glow intensity

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: endpoint and link state remain visible in thumbnail

assets.maximumVisualNoise: very low; no particle soup or generic neon magic
assets.animationPosture: static reference plus notes for future pulse cadence
assets.mipDownscalePosture: future link treatment must survive 0.5x viewport scale

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current vector endpoints, links, pulses, and visibility modes remain authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the Lume read as restrained land-power rather than generic spell VFX?
Are active, guide, contested, and severed states visually separable?
Does the link avoid cluttering units and capture rings?
assets.notes: One Lume link only; not a full Lume-network visual overhaul.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 16:9 campaign-map frame reference; not a new map
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future UI/background candidate only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: flat campaign-shell composition with route, selected mission, locked state, and return-from-Results posture

safeCrop.rule: selected-node panel and route cards stay fully visible

assets.silhouetteRule: mission nodes, route strokes, selected state, locked state, and chapter banner must read independently

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: selected Salto mission and route structure remain visible

assets.maximumVisualNoise: low behind node text and route labels
assets.mipDownscalePosture: future campaign elements must remain readable at 1366x768

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current campaign shell CSS/DOM route and node frames remain authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does this support a Salto mission launch without implying a new campaign map?
Are selected and locked states clear?
Does the campaign frame style align with HUD and Results?
assets.notes: Frame only; no new nodes, unlock rules, rewards, or campaign progression.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 4:3 concept sheet with front three-quarter pose, side silhouette, thumbnail, and material callouts

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: worker differs from Militia and Ranger without label support

assets.maximumVisualNoise: low on hands/tools; medium on cloth only if silhouette stays clean
assets.animationPosture: static concept only; build/repair animation deferred
assets.mipDownscalePosture: tool angle and lantern accent must survive 64px thumbnail

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current Worker vector silhouette remains authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the Worker read as utility/build/repair without modern-construction drift?
Are hands, tools, and pack anatomy coherent?
Is the silhouette distinct from Militia and Ranger?
assets.notes: Worker is first Barrosan readability proof after reference frames.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 4:3 hero concept sheet with front three-quarter pose, side silhouette, thumbnail, and material callouts

assets.transparencyRequirement: neutral sheet allowed; future runtime crop requires transparent background

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 4:3 building concept sheet with front three-quarter, footprint thumbnail, RTS silhouette, and material callouts
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future transparent building candidate sheet only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: isometric-friendly three-quarter with clear entrance and broad base
assets.transparencyRequirement: neutral sheet allowed; future runtime crop requires transparent background
assets.pivot: future runtime crop pivot at footprint center-bottom

safeCrop.rule: roofline, hearth/chimney, entrance, and footprint remain fully visible

assets.silhouetteRule: broad granite hall must read wider and more stable than tall

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: Command Hall reads larger and more central than Barracks

assets.maximumVisualNoise: medium-low; hearth windows can glow but wall texture must not blur footprint
assets.animationPosture: static concept only; construction/damage states deferred
assets.mipDownscalePosture: entrance and roofline must survive 96px thumbnail

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current Command Hall placeholder building remains authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the building read as a practical Barrosan command hall instead of a castle?
Are entrance, footprint, roofline, and team ownership clear?
Does the scale leave room for Barracks, mine, and shrine?
assets.notes: Building concept only; no construction rules, stronghold rules, or save fields change.

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 4:3 building concept sheet with footprint, shrine landmark, and restrained Lume/hearth material callouts

assets.sourceConceptFormat: 4:3 contrast sheet with 3-4 Ashen silhouettes, one commander/elite cue, and material callouts
assets.runtimeCandidateFormat: future transparent unit candidate sheet only after separate gate
assets.cameraRule: orthographic-feeling lineup; no splash-art composition or action blur
assets.transparencyRequirement: neutral sheet allowed; future runtime crop requires transparent background
assets.pivot: future runtime crop pivots at feet center per silhouette

safeCrop.rule: all silhouettes and material callouts remain visible

assets.silhouetteRule: Ashen must contrast Barrosan through disciplined flame and blackened iron, not demon/orc/undead shorthand

rtsScaleThumbnailSize.rule: raider, brute, hexer, and commander contrast against Barrosan units

assets.maximumVisualNoise: medium-low; flame accents must not erase body shape
assets.animationPosture: static contrast only; combat/VFX animation deferred
assets.mipDownscalePosture: blackened-iron shapes must not collapse into pure black at 64px

fallbackBehavior.behavior: current Ashen placeholder silhouettes remain authoritative

humanApprovalQuestions: Does the sheet contrast Barrosan without protected or generic monster drift?
Are Raider, Brute, Hexer, and Commander silhouettes separable?
Does fire/iron detail remain readable at small scale?
assets.notes: One enemy contrast sheet only; not a new faction, roster, or balance pass.

dependencyOrder.notes: Results frame is a required v0.107 coverage item and a companion to the HUD frame; it does not approve a separate UI kit.
Human review may choose runtime candidates, but v0.107 does not approve loading them.
A future explicit milestone must process, crop, validate, and integrate runtime art.

notes: All asset IDs are v0.107 planning IDs and map to one or more v0.105/v0.88 registry IDs through registryAssetIds.
All runtimeSlotIds are known v0.106 slots and remain fallback-owned.
No image generation, image import, runtime path assignment, gameplay change, balance change, save change, ID rename, new map, new faction, desktop port, or engine decision is approved.
